critical_data_integrity_alert.description: Multiple established Russia_UKR DOMAIN_SPECIALISTs (9 traders profiled in June 10 run) have lost Pool C membership (geo_accuracy_pool set to 0) because their traders.geo_resolved_trades_count now shows 4-5 records — below the Pool C minimum of 5. However, actual geo resolved trades in the DB range from 1,876 to 2,028 for these traders. The geo_elo column is also massively inflated: 0xbbd22b was 2953 (Jun 10) and is now 5469 — an apparent 85% inflation. This pattern affects all established Russia_UKR specialists.
critical_data_integrity_alert.hypothesis: The geo_resolved_trades_count column is being calculated from a subset of geo trades (possibly only post-April-28 trades or only trades meeting some new filter) rather than all historical geo trades. The geo_elo is then calculated from only those 4-5 trades, producing an inflated ELO from a very small sample. This would explain both the low geo_resolved_trades_count AND the inflated geo_elo simultaneously.
critical_data_integrity_alert.impact: Pool C dropped from ~2,851 to 2,157 (below alert threshold of 2,500). These 9 DOMAIN_SPECIALIST profiles are among the most valuable in the system for Russia_UKR signal generation. Their pool membership is likely incorrect — they should still qualify for Pool C based on actual trade history.
critical_data_integrity_alert.action_required: Oscar to investigate geo_resolved_trades_count and geo_elo calculation pipeline. Likely cause: update_geo_elo.py or update_research_exclusions.py is counting from a wrong subset. Fix should restore these traders to Pool C and recalculate geo_elo from full trade history. Do NOT adjust profiles until fix is confirmed — profiles reflect the correct archetype assignments from the full trade history.
